methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic, a Series B hypersonic propulsion company led by Blake Scholl, Joe Wilding, and Josh Krall, is currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $700M+ in total funding. The supersonic and hypersonic sector comprises a nascent peer universe of two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Hermeus, Venus Aerospace), though this narrow set limits traditional benchmarking rigor.

Given the sparse comparable universe within the exact stage and subsector, a peer-median approach is not reliably applicable. Instead, valuation is anchored to stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $1.5B last post-money valuation—a typical step-up coefficient for Series B advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $2.9B.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $2.4B to $3.3B, centered on $2.9B. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficiently granular peer comparables within the hypersonic segment and execution risk inherent to pre-revenue aerospace-grade development programs. The stage-progression method provides a disciplined benchmark but should be stress-tested against regulatory pathway clarity, capital requirements through certification, and resolution of technical milestones before material financing events.
